channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump can begin deportations of Syrian, Haitian TPS holders, Supreme Court says
item.description: Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.
item.content:encoded:
Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Trump urges GOP to prioritize election bill as his Iran war handling draws scrutiny
item.description: President Trump visited Capitol Hill Wednesday and faced off with Senate Republicans upset about his handling of the Iran War, after scrapping plans to sign a bipartisan housing affordability bill.
item.content:encoded: President Trump visited Capitol Hill Wednesday and faced off with Senate Republicans upset about his handling of the Iran War, after scrapping plans to sign a bipartisan housing affordability bill.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Political strategist on the growing tensions between Trump and Senate Republicans
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with political strategist Alex Conant about President Trump's tense meeting with Republican senators on Capitol Hill.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with political strategist Alex Conant about President Trump's tense meeting with Republican senators on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Judge blocks Postal Service proposal to restrict mail-in voting under Trump's order
item.description: A judge has blocked the U.S. Postal Service's proposals responding to President Trump's order, including not delivering ballots in states that don't turn over voter lists to the federal government.
item.content:encoded: A judge has blocked the U.S. Postal Service's proposals responding to President Trump's order, including not delivering ballots in states that don't turn over voter lists to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Death toll expected to rise after twin earthquakes hit Venezuela
item.description: Venezuela's acting president has declared a state of emergency after two powerful earthquakes struck the country Wednesday evening, killing at least 32. The death toll is expected to rise.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela's acting president has declared a state of emergency after two powerful earthquakes struck the country Wednesday evening, killing at least 32. The death toll is expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Eager for a break, farmers don't expect much relief after the Iran peace deal
item.description: Fertilizer prices have gone down with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the prospect of a U.S.-Iran peace deal. But struggling American farmers won't likely see relief for months.
item.content:encoded:
Fertilizer prices have gone down with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the prospect of a U.S.-Iran peace deal. But struggling American farmers won't likely see relief for months.

(Image credit: Kirk Siegler)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Norman Rockwell art that ignited a lawsuit and a love story is now on public view
item.description: So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.
item.content:encoded:
So You Want to See the President! Click to enlarge.'/>

So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.

(Image credit: Bruce M. White)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: At least 32 killed and 700 injured after two earthquakes hit Venezuela, Trump and Senate GOP face off in tense meeting, a top general is expected to retire, joining a number of Pentagon shakeups.
item.content:encoded: At least 32 killed and 700 injured after two earthquakes hit Venezuela, Trump and Senate GOP face off in tense meeting, a top general is expected to retire, joining a number of Pentagon shakeups.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: As 'The Bear' returns, real-life restauranteurs weigh in on the Michelin star quest
item.description: A Chicago restaurateur's desperate effort to secure a Michelin star comes to a head in the final season of FX's "The Bear." Real-life restaurant owners share whether or not it's worth the effort.
item.content:encoded: A Chicago restaurateur's desperate effort to secure a Michelin star comes to a head in the final season of FX's "The Bear." Real-life restaurant owners share whether or not it's worth the effort.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Iran's World Cup team navigates U.S. restrictions and pressure from Tehran
item.description: Iran's World Cup team says it's the most oppressed team due to U.S.-imposed visa and travel restrictions. But those hassles pale in comparison to the pressures Iran places on its own athletes.
item.content:encoded: Iran's World Cup team says it's the most oppressed team due to U.S.-imposed visa and travel restrictions. But those hassles pale in comparison to the pressures Iran places on its own athletes.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani
